items.label: schema-dev
messages: Now let me check the base template's constants to understand the export pattern:
Good, I understand the pattern. Now let me look at the existing utils to see if there's already a utils folder or pattern:
Now let me check if the `config/default.js` is syntactically valid before I make any changes:

Now I have full context. Let me implement the shared constants and utility functions.  **Intent: The Quick View feature is a modal overlay on existing PLP pages — no new routes or API config changes needed. My deliverables as schema-dev are: (1) shared constants for test IDs and configuration, (2) a utility function for product eligibility checking.**
Now create the shared utility function:
Now create a unit test for the utility:
Now let me validate the config still loads correctly and run the unit test:
All 12 tests pass. Let me verify the files I created:
Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The schema-dev phase is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## Schema-Dev Deliverables for `product-quick-view`  ### Files Modified | File | Change | |---|---| | `overrides/app/constants.js` | Added 3 Quick View constants | | `overrides/app/utils/quick-view.js` | **NEW** — `isQuickViewEligible()` utility | | `overrides/app/utils/quick-view.test.js` | **NEW** — 12 unit tests (all passing) |  ### Shared Constants Added | Export | Type | Value | Purpose | |---|---|---|---| | `QUICK_VIEW_TEST_IDS` | Object | `{ button, modal, spinner }` | Centralized test IDs for components + E2E | | `QUICK_VIEW_MODAL_SIZE` | String | `'4xl'` | Chakra Modal size for the dialog | | `QUICK_VIEW_EXCLUDED_PRODUCT_TYPES` | Array | `['set', 'bundle']` | Product types excluded from Quick View |  ### Utility Function **`isQuickViewEligible(product)`** — Pure function that returns `false` for: - `null`/`undefined` products - Products without a `productId` - Product sets (`type.set === true`) - Product bundles (`type.bundle === true`)  ### What Was NOT Changed (by design) - **Routes** (`routes.jsx`) — Quick View is a modal overlay on existing PLP pages, not a new route - **Commerce API config** (`config/default.js`) — Reuses existing SLAS/SCAPI configuration - **Validation hook** (`validate-app.sh`) — No new routes to validate  ### Contract for Downstream Agents The `storefront-dev` agent should import from: ```js import {QUICK_VIEW_TEST_IDS, QUICK_VIEW_MODAL_SIZE} from '../../constants' import {isQuickViewEligible} from '../../utils/quick-view' ```

shellCommands.command: cat > /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/constants.js << 'CONSTANTS_EOF' ↵ /* ↵ * Copyright (c) 2023, Salesforce, Inc. ↵ * All rights reserved. ↵ * SPDX-License-Identifier: BS
cat > /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/utils/quick-view.js << 'UTIL_EOF' ↵ /* ↵ * Copyright (c) 2024, Salesforce, Inc. ↵ * All rights reserved. ↵ * SPDX-License-Identifier:

cat > /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/utils/quick-view.test.js << 'TEST_EOF' ↵ /* ↵ * Copyright (c) 2024, Salesforce, Inc. ↵ * All rights reserved. ↵ * SPDX-License-Identif
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh all "feat(config): add Quick View shared constants and eligibility utility ↵ - Add QUICK_VIEW_TEST_IDS, QUICK_VIEW_MODAL_SIZE,
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view schema-dev 2>&1
